action_items.task: Abide by intrastate travel restrictions (do not cross state lines while driving).

Required for the PO to document progress in the file.

action_items.task: If WIOA letter does not come in the mail, call Maria before the appointment for a printout.
action_items.context: Contingency plan to ensure client does not show up empty-handed.

action_items.task: Attend DOT physical and drug test at the clinic near the Co-op

critical_updates.details: Client is currently working 10-hour days driving an articulated dump truck for a contractor doing site prep through the Co-op. Getting overtime and saving money.
Client met with caseworker Maria at the workforce office to apply for a WIOA grant to cover Tri-State Trucking Academy tuition. Classes start next month. Waiting on official approval letter.

Client reports feeling strong despite heavy labor. Blood pressure is down (recently checked at 128/82). Taking daily medication and stopped drinking energy drinks. DOT physical scheduled for next Thursday.
Client reports no drug use, but admits to having one beer on Sundays with dinner. Drug test scheduled to happen with the DOT physical next week.
No police contact reported. Client was reminded of the standard condition restricting travel strictly to intrastate.
Client reports that everything at home is 'very quiet' and 'good'.

next_episode_context: Client is currently working long hours doing site prep through the Co-op and saving money. He is waiting on a WIOA grant approval letter to begin CDL classes at Tri-State Trucking Academy next month. Health has improved, and he is preparing for a DOT physical next Thursday. The next office visit is scheduled for Tuesday the 14th at 2:00 PM, where he must present his WIOA approval and school enrollment documents.

acceptable_action_items.task: Continue taking blood pressure medication every morning
Document progress upon receipt of paperwork
